items.title: Loops and iterable traversal: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Use for...of for iterable values; for...in enumerates property keys and is unsuitable as ordinary array-value traversal.
for...in is the preferred way to iterate array values.

items.title: Loops and iterable traversal: executable evidence
items.prompt: Read a nonnegative integer n and print the sum from one through n using a terminating JavaScript iteration.
hints: Validate input, implement only the target behavior, and print exactly the declared output.

feedback.correct: All deterministic JavaScript behavior checks passed.
feedback.incorrect: At least one behavior check failed; inspect input, state, and output without revealing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Passes every normal and boundary runner test in the pinned JavaScript runtime.

answer.explanation: The reference implements the bounded Loops and iterable traversal behavior with exact deterministic output.
